# Rikugan (六眼)

> A reverse-engineering AI agent for IDA Pro. Rikugan is not an MCP client — it has its own agentic loop, in-process tool orchestration, streaming UI, multi-tab chat, session persistence, and MCP client support. It lives inside the disassembler.

## Key Concepts

- **Agent Loop**: Generator-based turn cycle (`rikugan/agent/loop.py`). User message → skill resolution → system prompt build → stream LLM → intercept tool calls → execute → feed results back → repeat.
- **Host**: IDA Pro (`rikugan_plugin.py`). Host-agnostic core in `rikugan/agent/`, `rikugan/core/`, `rikugan/ui/`, `rikugan/providers/`, `rikugan/mcp/`, `rikugan/skills/`, `rikugan/state/`.
- **Tools**: 60+ tools for IDA. Defined with the `@tool` decorator in `rikugan/ida/tools/`. Categories: navigation, functions, strings, database, disassembly, decompiler, xrefs, annotations, types, scripting, microcode.
- **Skills**: Markdown files with YAML frontmatter in `rikugan/skills/builtins/`. Activated with `/<slug>`. 12 built-in skills.
- **Exploration Mode**: 4-phase autonomous flow triggered by `/modify <goal>`: EXPLORE → PLAN → EXECUTE → SAVE. Phase 1 runs as a subagent.
- **Plan Mode**: `/plan <msg>` — generate plan, user approves, execute step by step.
- **Mutation Tracking**: Every mutating tool call is logged with a reverse operation. `/undo [N]` replays them in reverse.
- **MCP**: External MCP servers configured via `mcp.json`. Tools bridged into the registry with `mcp_<server>_<tool>` prefix.
- **Providers**: Anthropic (Claude), OpenAI, Google Gemini, Ollama, MiniMax, OpenAI-compatible endpoints.
- **Session Persistence**: Per-file auto-save/restore. Multi-tab chat. Session fork (deep copy to new tab).
- **Context Window**: Auto-compaction at 80% usage threshold.
- **Persistent Memory**: Central memory workspace per binary (SQLite structured facts + `MEMORY.md` manual notes) — facts saved by `save_memory` pseudo-tool, loaded into system prompt.

## Documentation

- [Full Architecture Reference](https://buzzer-re.github.io/rikugan/ARCHITECTURE.html): Agent loop internals, TurnEvent system, tool framework, exploration mode, plan mode, subagents, mutation tracking, context window, MCP integration, provider layer, UI layer, thread safety model.
- [Docs / Getting Started](https://buzzer-re.github.io/rikugan/docs.html): Installation, configuration, usage, skills, MCP setup.
- [ARCHITECTURE.md](ARCHITECTURE.md): Full technical reference (same content as Architecture page, local copy).
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): Developer guide — directory structure, how to add tools and skills.

## File Layout

```
rikugan/
├── agent/          # AgentLoop, BackgroundAgentRunner, exploration, plan, subagent, mutation, context window
├── core/           # Config, constants, errors, host context, logging, thread safety, types
├── ida/            # IDA Pro tools, UI, and tool registry
├── tools/          # Shared tool framework (@tool decorator, ToolDefinition, ToolRegistry)
├── providers/      # LLM provider integrations
├── mcp/            # MCP client (JSON-RPC 2.0, bridge to ToolRegistry)
├── skills/         # Skill discovery, loading, 12 built-in skills
├── state/          # SessionState, SessionHistory
├── ui/             # Shared Qt widgets (PanelCore, ChatView, MutationLogPanel, etc.)
rikugan_plugin.py           # IDA Pro entry point
```

## Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `/plan <msg>` | Plan mode: generate plan → approve → execute step by step |
| `/modify <msg>` | Exploration mode: EXPLORE → PLAN → EXECUTE → SAVE |
| `/explore <msg>` | Explore-only: autonomous read-only binary analysis |
| `/undo [N]` | Undo last N mutations (default 1) |
| `/memory` | Show current central memory contents (structured facts + `MEMORY.md` manual notes) |
| `/mcp` | Show MCP server health |
| `/doctor` | Diagnose setup issues |
| `/<skill-slug>` | Activate a skill (e.g. `/malware-analysis`, `/ctf`) |

## Built-in Skills

| Slug | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `/malware-analysis` | Windows PE malware triage, IOCs, MITRE ATT&CK |
| `/linux-malware` | ELF malware, packing, persistence |
| `/deobfuscation` | String decryption, CFF, opaque predicates, MBA |
| `/vuln-audit` | Buffer overflows, format strings, integer bugs |
| `/driver-analysis` | Windows kernel drivers, DispatchTable, IOCTL |
| `/ctf` | CTF challenge solving |
| `/generic-re` | General-purpose binary analysis |
| `/ida-scripting` | IDAPython scripting — fetch official hex-rays.com docs first, verified snippets for stable tasks, offline reference fallback |
| `/modify` | Exploration mode skill for binary modification |
| `/smart-patch-ida` | IDA-specific binary patching workflow |

## Adding Tools

```python
from typing import Annotated
from rikugan.tools.base import tool

@tool(category="navigation", mutating=False)
def my_tool(address: Annotated[str, "Target address (hex)"]) -> str:
    """Tool description."""
    ...
```

Register by adding the module to the host's `create_default_registry()` in `rikugan/ida/tools/registry.py`.

## Adding Skills

Create `rikugan/skills/builtins/<slug>/SKILL.md`:

```markdown
---
name: My Skill
description: One-line description
tags: [analysis]
allowed_tools: [decompile_function, rename_function]
---
Task: <instruction for the agent>
```
